taxonomicNotes: The Holotype of this species was collected together with Paratopotypes by Robert Miller and others in February 1976 from the Río (Potrero) Grande, about 9.6km W of the town of Ameca. Several Paratypes go back to collections from the type location in 1957 and 1971, one single male to a collection of R. Vrijenhoek and M. Douglas from a bridge over the Río Ameca (precisely: Río Cuisillos), 1km E of the estación Pacana, SE of the Presa La Vega, in 1981. No subpopulations are distinguished.

The types Kingston used for her description of Skiffia francesae (1978) were collected by Miller and Fitzsimons in 1970 from the Río Teuchitlán E of Teuchitlán. Two decades later, in 2006, a stock of Skiffia was found in the Molino pond near Cuyacapán, Laguna de Sayula drainage. Fish from this locality are regarded to belong to the Golden Skiffia. According to these two drainages, two subpopulations are recognized: The Upper Río Ameca subpopulation (type subpopulation) and the Laguna de Sayula subpopulation. Both are regarded Extinct in the Wild.
